results.rendered_html:
dahara 1〉 adj. klein, fein: atha yadasminbrahmapure daharaṃ puṇḍarīkaṃ veśma daharo 'sminnantarākāśaḥ  CHĀND. UP. 8,1,1 .  Ind. St. 2,182 . daharavidyā  COLEBR. Misc. Ess. I, 326 . hṛdaya  BHĀG. P. 10,81 _im_ ŚKDR.  kauśaṃ vāsaḥ — caṇḍātakaṃ daharaṃ vā fein  KĀTY. ŚR. 14,5,3 . jung an Jahren, im Gegens. zu vṛddha  VYUTP. 101 .  SADDH. P. 4,21,b . = ḍimba  H. an. 3,562 . = bālaka  MED. r. 166 .
— 2〉 m.
a〉 ein jüngerer Bruder.
— b〉 Maus  H. an.   MED. 
— Vgl. dabhra, dahra.

results.rendered_html:
dahara a. [dah-ar]
1 Small, subtile, fine, thin.
2 Young in age.
3 Unintelligible.
— raḥ
1 A child, an infant.
2 Any young animal.
3 A younger brother.
4 The cavity of the heart, or the heart itself.
5 A mouse or rat.
